turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: What was to speak in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablar. I spoke:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablé. And he spoke/she spoke/you (formal) spoke:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Habló. Good. We spoke is hablamos, which, I’m sure you’ve noticed, is exactly the same as the present, we speak. So, hablamos means we speak or we spoke, which, for one side is wonderful because it means you get another version of this very irregular tense without having to remember a new one. But, of course, it can cause some ambiguity, and in real life it can cause some ambiguity, not often, mind, because most of the time you have some context showing that you are talking about the past. For example, the word for when is cuando.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: So, if you want to say when we speak I understand everything, what was to understand?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Comprender. So, when we speak, I understand everything: S: Cuando hablamos, comprendo todo.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Cuando hablamos, comprendo todo. So, cuando hablamos, that bit could be when we speak, generally, or when we spoke, so that second half brings us into the context and we understand that when we speak, generally speaking, I understand everything. Cuando hablamos, comprendo todo. But, if you wanted to say when we spoke I understood everything, then, of course, the first bit is going to be the same, but that second bit, I understood everything, is going to give you that context. So, let’s practice that one - when we spoke I understood everything:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Very good. Cuando hablamos, comprendí todo. So again, the second part of that sentence is making it clear whether this hablamos is the present or the past. Cuando hablamos, comprendo todo; cuando hablamos, comprendí todo. We spoke yesterday. We spoke yesterday:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablamos ayer. Good. And we know that we can use the present with future context in Spanish. So, if you wanted to say we will speak tomorrow:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablamos mañana. So, hablamos ayer, we spoke yesterday; hablamos mañana, we will speak tomorrow. How would you say we wait a lot, we wait a lot, generally? What is a lot in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. To wait or to hope:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Esperar. If you forget this one you can think of desperate, which means “un-hoped”. Desesperado in Spanish, again one of these -ado/-ido adjectives from the version that comes after haber. You can see desesperar – desesperado, which made desperate. And it’s desesperado in Spanish, not“desperado”, like we have some spanishisms, let’s say, that have entered English, but they can enter incorrectly, like “desperado” is desesperado in Spanish; or in English you might hear “no problemo”, this is loosely based on Spanish, but of course, problem in Spanish is problema, not“problemo”, and actually you would say, not “no problema”. So, ningun problema esperar, to wait or to hope. So, talking generally, we wait a lot, we wait around a lot:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Esperamos mucho. Maybe you meant yesterday, here it might be ambiguous, unless the context is clear. So, if you want to clarify, you might want to say the word yesterday. How would you say we waited a lot yesterday?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Esperamos mucho ayer. What was to eat?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Comer. And what is we eat?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Comemos. We ate is comimos, comimos. We ate:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No comimos. What was to live?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And we live (generally):

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And we lived:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vivimos. So, what this means is that because of this one difference we have in the entire language between -er and -ir verbs, in the present we have -emos for -er and -imos for -ir, what that means –er is that when we go into the past, -ir verbs stay the same, vivimos - vivimos, but verbs are different: comemos - comimos. So, what was to see?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And we see, in the present?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vemos. And we saw:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vimos. Good. What was to go out or to leave?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. We are going out, or we are leaving:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. And we went out or we left:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: The same. What was to lose?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Perder. We lose:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. We lost:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say we got lost or we lost ourselves?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Nos perdimos. Good. Okay, so how would you say yesterday? First thing - what was the word for always?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Siempre. So, how would you say we always get lost? Siempre perdemos… Siempre nos perdemos

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Siempre nos perdemos, or nos perdemos siempre. But:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: : No perdimos… No nos perdimos ayer

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Nos perdemos siempre, pero no nos perdimos ayer. Very good. There we see in that sentence, perdemos for present and perdimos for past. But, of course, with -ir verbs, they have we don’t go out often, but we went out the same version for both. So, we could say, for example, yesterday. And often is a menudo, a menudo. Menudo is like a literary word that's not used very often for small, related to minute, actually, or miniscule, it’s not used very often in that sense, in we don’t go out often the sense of small. But a menudo is used very often to mean often. So,:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: But we went out yesterday:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good, very good. So, here we have the present and the past in this sentence and we see that both. Let’s make a recap. So, let’s do of them are salimos. No salimos a menudo, pero salimos ayer it with hablar and comer, which we will use as our hook verbs. So, I spoke:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: He spoke:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: We spoke:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And comer is to eat. So, I ate:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: He ate:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: We ate:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Very good.
